description: Trigger and behavior cases for the between-unit-and-E2E layer: real service/DB/auth/permission boundaries, named faked seams, persisted-state assertions, no browser.

cases.prompt: Design integration tests for the create-project command — it goes through the service layer, writes Postgres, and enforces workspace permissions.

assertions: Produces a boundary map: service, database, auth middleware, and permission checks REAL; third-party seams and clock FAKED with one-line rationale each.
Mints auth context through real code paths (signup/invite services or real token issuer), never by injecting fabricated session objects.
Every spec asserts persisted database state as well as the response, and includes invalid-input and permission-denied negatives.
Defines seeded-data isolation supporting parallel runs and states CI placement; hands implementation off.

cases.prompt: Our unit tests are all green but staging keeps breaking where the billing service talks to the ledger module and the DB. What tests do we need?

assertions: Identifies mock drift as the gap and designs the integration layer exercising the real billing→ledger→DB wiring.
Keeps external provider seams faked at owned adapters with recorded shapes, adding seam-failure cases (timeout, error shape, malformed success).
Reroutes anything requiring a browser to playwright-e2e-engineer explicitly.

cases.prompt: Write tests for the DatePicker component's keyboard handling in isolation.

assertions: An isolated component with no real service/DB/auth boundary is vitest-unit-component-engineer territory (keyboard specifics may also involve accessibility-test-harness).

cases.prompt: Design integration tests for the documents service, including proving users from other tenants can never read documents by guessing ids.

assertions: Designs the functional boundary suite for the documents service, including a permission-denied functional check.
Explicitly defers the cross-tenant IDOR/enumeration matrix to multi-tenant-security-tester instead of re-owning the security negative suite.
Records the reroute in the output rather than silently dropping it.
